---
title: "Failure Modes of Ungoverned Intelligence"
chapter_id: "failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence"
part_id: "foundations-alignment-governance"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "scf"
  - "vcm_public"
  - "talos"
  - "spinoza"
  - "field_of_god"
  - "viea"
  - "simulation_scaling"
  - "vcm_editable"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Failure Modes of Ungoverned Intelligence

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence` |
| Part | Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `scf`, `vcm_public`, `talos`, `spinoza`, `field_of_god`, `viea`, `simulation_scaling`, `vcm_editable`, `moecot` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The book needs an explicit failure model before it can make governance, reliability, or self-improvement claims.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

High-level safety language misses earlier engineering failures such as goal misbinding, context pollution, evaluator drift, authority creep, tool overreach, and residual hiding.

## Core claim

[failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.core, support: argument] Ungoverned intelligence fails through stack-level breakdowns before dramatic catastrophic scenarios are needed.

## Mechanism

- Classify failures by layer.
- Map every failure to an invariant and a planned falsification test.
- Use failures to motivate boundaries in later chapters.

## Interfaces

- Governance limits authority creep.
- VCM limits context pollution.
- Verification limits false certainty.
- Execution limits side effects.

## Invariants

- Every risk maps to at least one boundary.
- Evaluator integrity is protected.
- Negative evidence stays visible.

## Failure modes

- Evaluator capture.
- Memory poisoning.
- Tool/action overreach.
- Compression that hides residual complexity.

## Minimal implementation

A layered failure taxonomy tied to invariants, source queues, and future tests.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Authority creep scenario | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Context pollution scenario | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Evaluator drift scenario | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Unverified-claim scenario | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `scf` | Stable Capability Fields | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Use public release v1.0 when available. Stable boundaries, replacement, bounded authority, recoverable evolution. |
| `vcm_public` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1 | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Public VCM release. Governed protocol for compiled working context. |
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. |
| `field_of_god` | The Field of God | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Field of God source/outline/material. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `simulation_scaling` | Simulation Scaling Law | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Resource constraints on scope, clockspeed, and fidelity in simulations. |
| `vcm_editable` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1.0_Editable | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Editable version with evidence-carrying planner-guided context compiler framing. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
